item.title: Junior Staff Post - Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk Team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Reading this SCP brought to mind another very similar SCP. SCP-2470 is also a consciousness which, when it learns of or infers the existence of concepts close to it, negatively acts upon them. These two anomalies are very similar in that regard, and could be crosslinked.

Suggesting changing this:

While the method of this detection is unknown, it appears that SCP-3394 is only able to detect things which it has been able to logically deduce the existence of.

To this:

While the method of this detection is unknown, it appears that SCP-3394 is only able to detect things which it has been able to logically deduce the existence of1.

If anyone has any other ideas, feel free to suggest them below.

Footnotes
1. Investigation of similarities between the mechanism of this detection and that of SCP-2470 are underway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I reread the section and found myself agreeing entirely. I've added it directly to the description and it flows much more nicely.
Good eye, thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good stuff!

Small complaint though: Footnote 2 should be part of the main body rather than a footnote as its a pretty important part of the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to see you back on the saddle, Jade.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, that was pretty exciting. The containment procedures in particular are excellent, and this overall has the aesthetic of a really good Series I/II article. :) I love how the very bizarre specificity of what this can effect is eventually explained, while keeping to an obvious internal logic throughout.
This is an effective mystery without using any REDACTED or BLACK. +1. I have so many questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very creative it is always nice to see something that doesn’t sound like something else that has been done 100 times. Also enjoy the scientific paper-esque Results/Analysis organization.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All staff entering SCP-3394's containment cell are to maintain a threatening mindset and are encouraged to frequently picture themselves destroying lumps of brain matter.

I love skips that have special containment procedures that by themselves make you interested in what you're about to read

+1

One small complaint though

constructed using dovetail joints

I would personally have the footnote following this briefly describe a dovetail joint because I don't think the average pencil pushing researcher would know a craft term like that. Otherwise this is fine

item.title: Thank you,
item.content:encoded: Sincere thanks to Doctor Cimmerian, Veiedhimaedhr, DrChandra, and everyone else who helped revise this and provided incredible ideas.
